attributes.description:
This visualization displays a time-series trend of overall security events. [cite_start]This serves as a primary measure of performance, showing the volume of security-relevant activities over time[cite: 3].

**Why this visualization works:** It provides a high-level overview of the operational tempo and helps identify periods of unusual activity that may require reporting. [cite_start]A sudden spike or a sustained increase in events can be a key performance indicator (KPI) for security reports [cite: 4][cite_start].

**How an analyst can use it:** Monitor the trend for significant deviations from the baseline[cite: 5]. [cite_start]Use the time range selector to drill down into specific periods of interest[cite: 5]. [cite_start]This chart is a foundational element for any security performance report, providing context for more detailed metrics[cite: 6].

query.query: ((file.path:("/var/log/audit/*" OR "C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\*") OR file.extension:("log" OR "evtx" OR "json" OR "xml" OR "conf" OR "cfg" OR "ini") OR process.name:("auditd" OR "wevtutil" OR "auditpol" OR "journalctl" OR "powershell.exe" OR "cmd.exe" OR "bash" OR "wmic" OR "sc.exe" OR "reg.exe" OR "net.exe" OR "taskkill.exe" OR "schtasks.exe" OR "at.exe" OR "psexec.exe") OR event.module:("auditbeat" OR "winlogbeat" OR "filebeat" OR "sysmon" OR "osquery" OR "zeek" OR "suricata") OR cloud.service.name:("CloudTrail" OR "Azure Activity Logs" OR "GCP Audit Logs" OR "Kubernetes")) AND (event.category:("file" OR "process" OR "registry" OR "network" OR "iam" OR "configuration" OR "authentication" OR "malware" OR "intrusion_detection") OR event.type:("access" OR "modification" OR "deletion" OR "creation" OR "change" OR "start" OR "end" OR "info" OR "error" OR "connection" OR "protocol" OR "user" OR "group" OR "policy" OR "service_control" OR "execution" OR "rename" OR "permission_change" OR "ownership_change"))) AND NOT ((user.name:("system" OR "administrator" OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR "LocalSystem" OR "NetworkService" OR "*$" OR "org_admin_user") OR user.group.name:("Domain Admins" OR "org_admin_group")) AND event.outcome:success)

attributes.title: [CCI-000211] Overall Security Event Trend (converted)

attributes.description:
This visualization displays the maximum anomaly score over time for the job monitoring reportable incidents.

**Why this visualization works:** It provides a timeline of when potentially reportable incidents are occurring at an anomalous rate. This is a crucial leading indicator, helping analysts proactively identify and investigate clusters of activity that may need to be formally reported, ensuring compliance with CCI-000211.

**How an analyst can use it:** Monitor this chart for spikes in the anomaly score. Any significant increase warrants a deeper look. Use the time selector to zoom in on the anomaly, then use the 'Top Anomaly Influencers' table to identify the specific users, hosts, and actions driving the unusual activity.
attributes.title: [PM-6, CCI-000211] ML - Reportable Incidents Anomaly Score Trend

attributes.description:
This visualization displays the maximum anomaly score over time for the job monitoring security events.

**Why this visualization works:** It provides a clear timeline of when anomalous spikes in security events are occurring. Instead of relying on fixed thresholds, the ML model identifies unusual increases relative to the learned baseline, helping to detect subtle but significant incidents that might otherwise be missed.

**How an analyst can use it:** Look for spikes in the anomaly score. A high score (e.g., above 75) indicates a significant deviation from normal activity. Use the time selector to isolate these periods and cross-reference with the 'Top Anomaly Influencers' table to see which hosts, users, or rules are associated with the anomaly.
attributes.title: [PM-6, CCI-000210, 000211] ML - Security Events Anomaly Score Trend

attributes.description:
This table provides a detailed breakdown of the entities influencing the anomaly scores for security events.

**Why this visualization works:** It moves beyond just knowing 'when' an anomaly occurred to understanding 'what' or 'who' caused it. By pinpointing the specific hosts, users, or processes contributing to an unusual spike in security events, it allows for rapid, targeted investigation.

**How an analyst can use it:** Use this table to identify the primary drivers behind any significant anomalies seen in the trend chart. Sort by the max anomaly score to prioritize the most impactful influencers. An analyst can then pivot their investigation by filtering on a specific host, user, or process to understand the full scope of the potential incident.
attributes.title: [PM-6, CCI-000210, 000211] ML - Top Security Event Anomaly Influencers

attributes.description: This section provides visualizations designed to be exported or reported on, helping to assess and communicate information security measures of performance, thereby satisfying CCI-000211. These visualizations summarize key security metrics, event trends, and anomaly detection results, which can be used to generate scheduled reports via Kibana's reporting features. To perform a component-level assessment, utilize the global filters at the top of the Kibana dashboard. Apply filters like 'host.name: <your_component_name>' to focus on a specific system's performance metrics.
attributes.title: [CCI-000211] Reporting on Information Security Measures of Performance
attributes.visState: {"title":"[CCI-000211] Reporting on Information Security Measures of Performance","type":"markdown","aggs":[],"params":{"markdown":"# [CCI-000211] Information Security Performance Reporting\n\n### Report on the results of information security measures of performance.","fontSize":12,"openLinksInNewTab":false}}

attributes.description: This visualization displays the health status of Information Security Performance Reporting for CCI-000211. The status is determined by the maximum anomaly score from associated Machine Learning jobs that detect unusual increases in key security events and reportable incidents. A high score indicates a significant deviation from the baseline, suggesting potential performance issues or threats that require investigation and reporting.
attributes.title: [CCI-000211] Reporting on Information Security Measures of Performance ML

attributes.description:
This visualization provides a quick summary of the total number of high-priority security incidents that would typically be included in a performance report[cite: 10]. [cite_start]This includes failed events and high-severity anomalies [cite: 10][cite_start].

**Why this visualization works:** It gives an immediate, high-level count of reportable incidents[cite: 11]. [cite_start]This single metric is a powerful KPI for security performance reporting, showing the volume of significant events that required attention [cite: 11][cite_start].

**How an analyst can use it:** Use this number as a headline metric in security reports[cite: 12]. [cite_start]Track this number over time to demonstrate the effectiveness of security controls[cite: 12]. [cite_start]A decreasing number may indicate improved security posture, while a spike would be a key finding to report and explain[cite: 13].

query.query: event.outcome:failure OR (event.dataset:*.anomaly AND anomaly_score >= 75) OR ((event.action:("user_password_changed" OR "file_deleted" OR "registry_key_deleted" OR "privilege-escalation" OR "critical-process-terminated") OR event.type:("deletion" OR "creation") OR (event.category:"authentication" AND event.type:"user")) AND event.outcome:success AND NOT (user.name:("system" OR "administrator" OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR "LocalSystem" OR "NetworkService" OR "*$" OR "org_authorized_user") OR user.group.name:("Domain Admins" OR "org_admin_group")))

attributes.title: [CCI-000211] Total Reportable Incidents

attributes.description:
This table provides a detailed breakdown of security events, which can be directly included in performance reports[cite: 7]. [cite_start]It summarizes event counts by host, user, and event category [cite: 7][cite_start].

**Why this visualization works:** It offers granular metrics that are essential for reporting on information security measures of performance[cite: 8]. [cite_start]It allows stakeholders to see where security events are originating from and what types of activities are most prevalent [cite: 8][cite_start].

**How an analyst can use it:** Use this table to identify top-talking hosts, users generating the most security events, and the most common event categories[cite: 9]. [cite_start]This information can be exported and included in regular security briefings and performance reports to demonstrate security posture and highlight areas of concern[cite: 9].

query.query: ((file.path:("/var/log/audit/*" OR "C:\Windows\System32\winevt\Logs\*") OR file.extension:("log" OR "evtx" OR "json" OR "xml" OR "conf" OR "cfg" OR "ini") OR process.name:("auditd" OR "wevtutil" OR "auditpol" OR "journalctl" OR "powershell.exe" OR "cmd.exe" OR "bash" OR "wmic" OR "sc.exe" OR "reg.exe" OR "net.exe" OR "taskkill.exe" OR "schtasks.exe" OR "at.exe" OR "psexec.exe") OR event.module:("auditbeat" OR "winlogbeat" OR "filebeat" OR "sysmon" OR "osquery" OR "zeek" OR "suricata") OR cloud.service.name:("CloudTrail" OR "Azure Activity Logs" OR "GCP Audit Logs" OR "Kubernetes")) AND (event.category:("file" OR "process" OR "registry" OR "network" OR "iam" OR "configuration" OR "authentication" OR "malware" OR "intrusion_detection") OR event.type:("access" OR "modification" OR "deletion" OR "creation" OR "change" OR "start" OR "end" OR "info" OR "error" OR "connection" OR "protocol" OR "user" OR "group" OR "policy" OR "service_control" OR "execution" OR "rename" OR "permission_change" OR "ownership_change"))) AND NOT ((user.name:("system" OR "administrator" OR "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" OR "LocalSystem" OR "NetworkService" OR "*$" OR "org_admin_user") OR user.group.name:("Domain Admins" OR "org_admin_group")) AND event.outcome:success)

attributes.title: [CCI-000211] Detailed Security Performance Metrics

attributes.description:
This table provides a detailed breakdown of the entities influencing the anomaly scores for reportable incidents.

**Why this visualization works:** It directly connects anomalies to the specific actors and actions involved. This is essential for incident response and reporting, as it provides the 'who, what, and where' needed to understand the scope and nature of a potential security incident quickly.

**How an analyst can use it:** When the trend chart shows a spike, this table is the next step. Identify the top influencing users, hosts, and event actions. For example, a high score associated with a specific user and the 'file_deleted' action on a critical host is a high-priority event that requires immediate investigation to determine if it meets the criteria for a reportable incident.
attributes.title: [PM-6, CCI-000211] ML - Top Reportable Incident Anomaly Influencers
